title: senecio fremontii subsp. blitoides
intro: Senecio fremontii subsp. blitoides, also known as Fremont's ragwort or Fremont's greasewood, is a subspecies of the Senecio fremontii plant, a shrubby perennial in the daisy family. It is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it beneficial for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Senecio fremontii subsp. blitoides include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide relief from skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health issues and promote overall well-being.
